data.description:
## Incident Overview

**Scenario Type**: Test authoring footgun
**Source**: /dso:end-session technical-learnings sweep, 2026-04-28 sprint session (epic 6f7a-4ba6)

### Expected Behavior
Heredoc-passed Python code inside a bash command substitution should execute cleanly.

### Actual Behavior
`py_output=$(python3 - "$REPO_ROOT" <<'PYEOF'...PYEOF)` produces 'unterminated here-document' warnings and shell parsing errors when the heredoc spans multiple lines inside a subshell.

### Root Cause
Bash's heredoc parsing inside command substitution ($(...)) is unreliable for multi-line scripts. The PYEOF delimiter may not be recognized correctly.

### Fix
Write the Python script to a temp file using mktemp then execute it:
  py_script=$(mktemp /tmp/bridge-test.XXXXXX.py)
  cat > "$py_script" << 'PYEOF'
  ...Python code...
  PYEOF
  python3 "$py_script" args

## Component
tests/acceptance/test-ticket-delete-e2e.sh, general bash test authoring
data.title: [bridge test fixtures]: heredoc-in-subshell $(python3 - <<'PYEOF') causes unterminated here-document error in bash tests
